user.title: Load Scheduler
user.description: Set up the hub that all scheduled loads will share. Point it at the sensor that publishes electricity prices with a forecast for at least the next day. You can add the sell-price and solar-production forecasts now or later; loads (water heater, dishwasher, EV, floor heating, …) are added afterwards from the integration's page.

data.name: Name
data.baseline_entity: Consumption sensor for the baseline profile (optional)
data.sell_threshold: Only divert when live sell is below this (€/kWh)

data_description.buy_price_entity: A sensor exposing today/tomorrow price slots in its attributes (e.g. Nord Pool, ENTSO-e, or a day-ahead template). Used to find the cheapest run times.
data_description.sell_price_entity: What you earn exporting 1 kWh. Enables buy-vs-sell solar arbitration: running on solar costs the foregone sell price.
data_description.solar_forecast_entity: Per-slot PV forecast sensor(s) (e.g. Solcast 'today' + 'tomorrow', whose 'detailedForecast' is read). Enables valuing slots with solar excess at the sell price.
data_description.consumption_baseline_w: Flat fallback for the always-on house load subtracted from the PV forecast to estimate exportable excess. Used when no baseline sensor is set or it has no statistics yet.
data_description.baseline_entity: A house-consumption power sensor (W). Its long-term statistics build an hour-of-day baseline that's subtracted from the PV forecast — more accurate than the flat value, and it needs no high-frequency history.
data_description.forecast_price_entity: A predictor sensor estimating prices BEYOND the real day-ahead horizon (e.g. day-after-tomorrow, from Finland wind + temperature + solar forecasts). Used only for slots later than the real prices, so a load with a multi-day horizon can bet on a forecast-cheaper window.
data_description.forecast_price_margin: Added to forecast prices so a load only defers to a forecast window when it's cheaper than the known prices by at least this much — your confidence in the forecast.
data_description.net_energy_entity: Enables real-time divert: when this shows live export beyond the threshold (and selling isn't worth it), surplus is dispatched to solar-enabled loads by priority.
data_description.predicted_net_energy_entity: Optional interval-aware gate. A sensor predicting the net energy by the end of the current metering interval (e.g. the 15-min net extrapolated to its close). When set, a load is only switched ON for divert when BOTH the live and the predicted net show export — so a load isn't started late in an interval when you won't still be exporting by its end.
data_description.live_sell_entity: When set, divert only runs while the live sell price is below the threshold (i.e. selling the surplus isn't worth more than self-consuming it).

reconfigure.title: Load Scheduler
reconfigure.description: Update the shared price and solar forecast sources.

data.name: Name
data.baseline_entity: Consumption sensor for the baseline profile (optional)
data.sell_threshold: Only divert when live sell is below this (€/kWh)

error.invalid_price_entity: That sensor doesn't expose a recognised price forecast (no today/tomorrow price list found in its attributes).

abort.already_configured: This price sensor is already configured.

init.title: Add a load
init.description: Configure how this load is scheduled into cheap hours. Leave the window empty to search the next 24 hours; set an earliest start and a deadline (e.g. 21:00 → 07:00) to constrain it.

data.name: Name
data.allow_solar: Value solar excess for this load

data_description.mode: Non-sequential = the cheapest scattered slots (pausable loads). Sequential = the cheapest contiguous block(s). Informational = compute and display only, never switch anything.
data_description.target_type: Runtime = minutes of run time. Energy = kWh to deliver at the load's power draw (e.g. EV charging); set the power draw too.
data_description.target_minutes: The target: minutes for a runtime load, or kWh for an energy load. Adjustable later via the load's number entity.
data_description.horizon_hours: Instead of a daily window, search the next N hours, so the load can defer an expensive day to a cheaper next day once tomorrow's prices are known. Minimum-service still prevents it running out.
data_description.runs_per_day: For sequential loads, how many separate blocks to schedule (e.g. run a washing machine twice).
data_description.min_service_minutes: A guaranteed minimum that runs even when prices are above the cap, so the load never starves.
data_description.min_run_minutes: Compressor protection: drop scheduled runs shorter than this.
data_description.min_off_minutes: Compressor protection: keep running through off-gaps shorter than this instead of short-cycling.
data_description.price_cap: Discretionary runtime above the minimum is only scheduled at or below this price.
data_description.controlled_entity: A switch / input_boolean the integration turns on and off with the schedule. Leave empty for informational loads.
data_description.failsafe_start: If the price forecast is ever unavailable, run for the target duration starting at this time so the load still runs.
data_description.delivered_entity: Optional override for 'already delivered today'. Leave empty and the integration measures it itself — the on-time of the feedback element (or the controlled switch) since local midnight — so dynamic-remaining works with no extra sensor. Set this only if you have a better signal (e.g. an energy meter or a predictor). A sensor's unit (hours, minutes, or kWh) is interpreted. Subtracted from the target so a load that already ran enough (e.g. on solar) is scheduled for less.
data_description.allow_solar: When the hub has a solar forecast, value slots with predicted excess at the sell price instead of the buy price.
data_description.coexist: Top-up mode: the scheduler only ever switches this load ON (cheap hours / solar / safety floor / minimum-service) and only switches it OFF for a run it started itself. It never turns off a run an external control (a comfort automation, or you flipping the switch) started, and credits that on-time as delivered. Use it to add cheap/green energy on top of existing control without fighting it (e.g. floor heating with comfort automations).
data_description.draw_kw: The load's power, used to value how much of a slot's solar excess it can actually absorb. Without it, any excess counts as fully solar.
data_description.priority: When several loads compete for the same solar surplus, higher-priority loads claim it first.
data_description.temp_entity: For comfort/secondary heat (e.g. floor heating): when this temperature drops below the threshold, the load is forced on regardless of price.
data_description.feedback_entity: Power (or on/off) of the heating element itself (e.g. an LVV LED detector). Used so a load that is already satisfied (running but idle) isn't given more solar.

price_unavailable.title: Load Scheduler: price forecast unavailable
price_unavailable.description: The configured price sensor ({entity}) is not providing a usable forecast, so no loads can be scheduled. Check that the sensor is available and exposes today/tomorrow prices in its attributes. Scheduling resumes automatically once it recovers.
